title: PluginAction enum carries 11 variants — Tell-Don't-Ask violation forces handle_plugin_action match table to grow with every feature
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/cli/repl/slash.rs:14-47, src/cli/repl/slash.rs:1100-1165
## Violation
PluginAction is a tagged-union of 11 variants and handle_plugin_action is a giant match that dispatches each to a free function. Adding a new plugin sub-command means: edit the enum, edit slash_plugin, edit handle_plugin_action, and write a new free function — OCP violation. Quoted lines:

    pub enum PluginAction {
        Menu, Help, Install { ... }, Manage, Uninstall { plugin: String },
        Enable { plugin: String }, Disable { plugin: String },
        Validate { path: Option<String> }, Marketplace { ... }, Reload,
        RunCommand { plugin_name: String, command_name: String },
    }
    ...
    pub fn handle_plugin_action(action: PluginAction, plugin_manager: &mut plugins::PluginManager) {
        match action {
            PluginAction::Menu => plugin_action_menu(plugin_manager),
            PluginAction::Help => plugin_action_help(),
            ...
        }
    }

Marketplace inside this enum carries its own (action, target) sub-enum-via-strings — a second-level switch buried in plugin_marketplace. The pattern repeats: a string-keyed sub-dispatcher pretending to be type-safe.
## Mandated refactor
Replace PluginAction with a `trait PluginCommand { fn name(&self) -> &str; fn run(&self, mgr: &mut PluginManager) -> Result<(), PluginError>; }` and register each variant as its own struct. slash_plugin's parser becomes a registry lookup. Same applies to the inner Marketplace dispatch — split into AddMarketplace, RemoveMarketplace, UpdateMarketplace, ListMarketplaces structs. handle_plugin_action shrinks to one polymorphic call.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
